idea.hook: Think the fastest human-made object is quick?
facts: The Parker Solar Probe zips at a record-breaking 195 km/s (430,000 mph) relative to the Sun.
At this incredible speed, reaching Mars (approx. 55 million km minimum distance) would still take over 3.2 days.
Now, imagine traveling at the ultimate speed limit – light speed (300,000 km/s) – to the Andromeda Galaxy, our nearest galactic neighbor, 2.5 million light-years distant.
idea.payoff: Even at light speed, it would STILL take 2.5 MILLION years to reach Andromeda! Human civilization as we know it would be dust countless times over.
idea.title: 🚀 How FAST is FAST in Space?! 🤯

scenes.text: Think the fastest human-made object is quick?
scenes.visual: A stylized abstract representation of extreme speed, like streaking light trails across a dark background.

scenes.text: Parker Solar Probe: 195 km/s (430,000 mph) relative to Sun.
scenes.visual: An animated Parker Solar Probe flying at high speed near the Sun (represented by a bright, fiery sphere). Speed lines emphasize motion.

scenes.text: At that speed, reaching Mars would still take over 3.2 days!
scenes.visual: Illustrations of Earth and Mars, with an arrow indicating the immense distance. A digital counter ticking up towards '3.2 Days'.

scenes.text: Now, imagine light speed (300,000 km/s) to Andromeda...
scenes.visual: A stylized representation of the Milky Way galaxy, which then quickly shrinks as the view zooms out to reveal the Andromeda Galaxy as a distant, larger spiral.

scenes.text: Even at light speed, 2.5 MILLION years. Human civilization: long gone.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking deep space view filled with countless galaxies and nebulae, emphasizing the vastness of the cosmos. A faint, distant Earth icon appears and fades quickly.

youtube.title: 🚀 How FAST is FAST in Space?! 🤯
